extraction.one_line_summary: CHICONE Giulio attacked early in breakaway, held off LANDA by 2:20 and THOMAS by 1:44 to take stage and yellow jersey
tactical_patterns: Trek failed to tell Chicone maintain tempo alone; hesitation asking for help nearly cost stage/yellow instead of riding solo
Ineos rode collected pace with no panic despite Landa threatening at 2:13 gap, suggesting calculated GC defense over stage
Thomas attacked Alaphilippe in final kilometer gaining 1:46, revealing Ineos targeting GC shake-up not just defending Bernal
Quintana presence in early moves suggested hidden form, yet commentary noted everyone focused on Ineos missing alternate threats

rider_signals.CHICONE Giulio: positive: first Tour, post-Giro, ignored for fatigue concerns yet took 2:20 over Landa proving Grand Tour double-race form
rider_signals.THOMAS Gareth: positive: attacked Alaphilippe in final km gaining 1:46, significantly distanced Bernal showing internal Ineos hierarchy shift
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: struggled and lost time to Thomas, Ineos riding collected suggests they're protecting Thomas not Bernal
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: chased at 2:13 gap but faded to 2:20, eyes bigger than stomach commentary suggests overestimated form
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: barely held yellow at 1:46 down, struggled final kilometer when Thomas attacked revealing summit weakness
rider_signals.THIBAUT Pinot: negative: third day unable to feature in leading group, reversal of fortune still absent contradicting French hope status
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: third consecutive day in leading group, commanding KOM lead suggesting sustained breakaway form
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: cracked at back of front group early, confirming declining form pattern
